title: betula alba var. cordifolia
intro: Betula alba var. cordifolia, also known as white birch, is a deciduous tree native to eastern Asia, characterized by its white, papery bark and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the bark of Betula alba var. cordifolia has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system. Betula alba var. cordifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its bark is also used in traditional herbal remedies such as salves and poultices.
